name: dealclaw-negotiate
description: AI negotiation agent with verifiable reasoning on Solana. Autonomously negotiates service pricing using anchoring, BATNA leverage, concession mapping, and tactic detection — with SHA-256 hashed reasoning chains for on-chain verifiability.
author: DealClaw

constructor.description: Initialize a new DealClawEngine instance for a specific deal.

dealId.description: Unique identifier for the negotiation deal.

service.description: Name or type of the service being negotiated.

budget.description: Maximum amount the buyer is willing to pay, in SOL.

walkAwayPrice.description: Absolute price ceiling beyond which the agent will terminate negotiation, in SOL.

marketData.description: Market pricing context used for anchoring and BATNA analysis.

avgPrice.description: Average market price for the service in SOL.

alternatives.description: List of alternative service providers with pricing.

price.description: Price offered by the alternative provider in SOL.

tools.name: processRound
tools.description: Process a single negotiation round against the vendor's current offer. Selects the optimal tactic (anchoring, BATNA leverage, concession mapping, or tactic detection) and returns a counter-offer with full reasoning chain and a SHA-256 reasoning hash suitable for on-chain attestation.

vendorOffer.description: The vendor's current price offer in SOL.

round.description: The current negotiation round number (1-based). Maximum 6 rounds before automatic walk-away.

tactic.description: The tactic applied this round (ANCHORING, BATNA_LEVERAGE, CONCESSION_MAPPING, DEAL_CLOSED, WALK_AWAY, or TACTIC_DETECTION:*).

counterOffer.description: The counter-offer in SOL, or null if walking away.

confidence.description: Confidence score for this decision (0.0 to 1.0).

reasoning.description: Ordered list of reasoning steps explaining the decision.

reasoningHash.description: SHA-256 hash of the reasoning array for on-chain verification.

vendorConcessionRate.description: Rate of vendor price movement between the last two offers.

gapToTarget.description: Difference between the latest vendor offer and the buyer's budget.

roundsRemaining.description: Number of rounds remaining before automatic walk-away.

walkAwayRisk.description: Probability estimate (0.0–1.0) that the deal will end in walk-away.

tools.name: getSummary
tools.description: Retrieve a complete summary of the negotiation, including configuration, current state, and the full history of all rounds with their decisions and reasoning.

tools.name: getReasoning
tools.description: Retrieve the reasoning chain for a specific negotiation round. Useful for auditing, dispute resolution, and on-chain verification against the stored reasoning hash.

round.description: The round number to retrieve reasoning for (1-based).

returns.description: Array of reasoning step strings for the given round, or null if the round has not been played.

tactics.description: Opens aggressively low at 65% of market average to set a favorable reference point.
Uses best-alternative-to-negotiated-agreement data to pressure the vendor when concession rate is low.
tactics.appliesIn: round 2+, or when vendor concession rate < 15%

tactics.description: Applies diminishing concessions (decay factor 0.6) while predicting the vendor's price floor.
Detects when vendor movement is below 2% and responds with a firm budget-ceiling counter.
Identifies artificial urgency signals and holds the current position.
Detects when headline price drops but effective value decreases, countering at the previous position.

examples.description: Basic 3-round negotiation for a Solana RPC service
